  "text": "The Grafana datasource plugin query path in grafana/grafana involves three major layers:\n\n## 1. Query Request Handler (HTTP Layer)\n\n**`pkg/api/ds_query.go`** is the entry point. The route `POST /ds/query` is registered in `pkg/api/api.go` (line 506) and mapped to `getDSQueryEndpoint()`. This function checks the `FlagQueryServiceRewrite` feature flag:\n- If enabled, it rewrites requests to `/apis/query.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/{namespace}/query` and serves them via the Kubernetes API machinery (`clientConfigProvider.DirectlyServeHTTP`).\n- Otherwise, it calls `QueryMetricsV2`, which binds the incoming HTTP body as a `dtos.MetricRequest` (defined in `pkg/api/dtos/models.go`), delegates to `queryDataService.QueryData` or `QueryDataNew`, and returns the response via `toJsonStreamingResponse` which streams JSON back to the client.\n\n## 2. Query Service Core Logic\n\n**`pkg/services/query/query.go`** contains the `ServiceImpl` which implements the `Service` interface. It:\n- `parseMetricRequest`: parses queries grouped by datasource UID, resolves datasource info (from cache or service), and builds `backend.DataQuery` objects.\n- `handleQuerySingleDatasource`: for single-datasource queries, builds a `backend.QueryDataRequest`, retrieves the plugin context via `plugincontext.Provider.GetWithDataSource`, and dispatches to the `plugins.Client`.\n- `executeConcurrentQueries`: for multi-datasource requests, fans out queries concurrently using `errgroup`.\n- `handleExpressions`: routes expression queries through the expression service (`expr.Service.TransformData`).\n- It supports two routing modes: single-tenant (direct plugin call) and query-service/multi-tenant (via `QSDatasourceClientBuilder` which calls the k8s query service API).\n\n**`pkg/services/query/models.go`** defines `parsedRequest` and `parsedQuery` (internal query parsing types).\n**`pkg/services/query/errors.go`** defines typed errors for the query path.\n\n## 3. Kubernetes Query API (Alternative/New Path)\n\nWhen `FlagQueryService` or `FlagQueryServiceRewrite` is enabled, the query is handled by:\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/register.go`**: `QueryAPIBuilder` + `RegisterAPIService` register the query API group with the Kubernetes API server.\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go`**: `queryREST.Connect` is the HTTP handler called by the apiserver. It binds the request as a `query.QueryDataRequest`, calls `handleQuery`, which rebuilds a `dtos.MetricRequest` and calls `service.QueryData` (the same core query service).\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/client/plugin.go`**: `pluginClient.QueryData` performs access-control checks and dispatches to the internal `plugins.Client` with the datasource's plugin context.\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/client/instance_provider.go`**: `singleTenantInstanceProvider` wires the single-tenant plugin client.\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/clientapi/clientapi.go`**: Defines the `QueryDataClient`, `Instance`, and `InstanceProvider` interfaces.\n- **`pkg/services/dsquerierclient/qs_datasource_client_builder.go`**: `QSDatasourceClientBuilder` interface and implementations that bridge between the query service and the datasource client.\n\n## 4. Plugin Backend Query Dispatch\n\n**`pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go`**: The `Service.QueryData` method looks up the plugin by ID from the plugin registry, validates it is not decommissioned, and calls `p.QueryData`.\n\n**Plugin implementations:**\n- **`pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/grpc_plugin.go`**: `grpcPlugin.QueryData` — dispatches to the gRPC client for external plugins.\n- **`pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_v2.go`**: `ClientV2.QueryData` — converts the request to protobuf via `backend.ToProto().QueryDataRequest()`, sends it over gRPC, and converts the response back via `backend.FromProto().QueryDataResponse()`.\n- **`pkg/plugins/backendplugin/coreplugin/core_plugin.go`**: `corePlugin.QueryData` — calls `QueryDataHandler.QueryData` directly for built-in plugins.\n- **`pkg/plugins/ifaces.go`**: The `Client` interface (alias for `backend.Handler`) that all plugin client implementations must satisfy.\n\n## 5. Plugin Middleware Chain\n\n**`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginsintegration.go`**: `CreateMiddlewares` + `NewMiddlewareHandler` assemble the middleware stack that wraps every plugin call:\n1. `TracingMiddleware` (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/tracing_middleware.go`) — creates OpenTelemetry spans.\n2. `MetricsMiddleware` (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/metrics_middleware.go`) — records Prometheus metrics (request count, duration, size).\n3. `ContextualLoggerMiddleware` — contextual logging.\n4. `TracingHeaderMiddleware` — propagates tracing headers.\n5. `ClearAuthHeadersMiddleware` — clears sensitive headers.\n6. `OAuthTokenMiddleware` — injects OAuth tokens.\n7. `CookiesMiddleware` — forwards cookies.\n8. `CachingMiddleware` (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/caching_middleware.go`) — cache hit/miss logic for query responses.\n9. `ForwardIDMiddleware`, `UseAlertHeadersMiddleware`, `UserHeaderMiddleware`, `HTTPClientMiddleware`.\n10. `ErrorSourceMiddleware` — classifies errors as plugin-source vs downstream.\n\n## 6. Query Response Transformation\n\n**`pkg/expr/transform.go`**: `Service.TransformData` handles expression evaluation — it builds a DAG from the queries, executes datasource queries to get raw frames, then applies math/reduce/resample expressions.\n\n**`pkg/expr/converter.go`**: `ResultConverter.Convert` converts raw data frames from datasource responses into `mathexp.Results` for use in the expression pipeline (handles Prometheus vectors, time-series, number tables, dataplane frames).\n\n**`pkg/expr/query_convert.go`**: `ConvertBackendRequestToDataRequest` converts `backend.QueryDataRequest` to the k8s `data.QueryDataRequest` format for the query service routing path.\n\n**`pkg/apis/query/v0alpha1/query.go`**: Defines `QueryDataRequest` and `QueryDataResponse` as k8s runtime objects, plus `GetResponseCode` for mapping response errors to HTTP status codes.\n\nThe full call chain for a standard single-datasource query is:\n`getDSQueryEndpoint → QueryMetricsV2 → ServiceImpl.QueryData → parseMetricRequest → handleQuerySingleDatasource → plugincontext.GetWithDataSource → [middleware chain] → plugins/manager/client.QueryData → grpcPlugin.QueryData → ClientV2.QueryData → (gRPC) → toJsonStreamingResponse`"

- **`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginsintegration.go`** — `CreateMiddlewares` assembles tracing, metrics, caching, auth, header middlewares

- **`pkg/expr/converter.go`** — `ResultConverter.Convert` for frame type conversion

	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/apimachinery/identity"

	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginstore"
	"github.com/grafana/grafana/pkg/setting"
)

const (
	pluginSettingsCacheTTL    = 5 * time.Second
	pluginSettingsCachePrefix = "plugin-setting-"
)

func ProvideService(cfg *setting.Cfg, cacheService *localcache.CacheService, pluginStore pluginstore.Store,
	dataSourceCache datasources.CacheService, dataSourceService datasources.DataSourceService,
	pluginSettingsService pluginsettings.Service, pluginRequestConfigProvider pluginconfig.PluginRequestConfigProvider) *Provider {
	return &Provider{
		BaseProvider:          newBaseProvider(cfg, pluginRequestConfigProvider),
		cacheService:          cacheService,
		pluginStore:           pluginStore,
		dataSourceCache:       dataSourceCache,
		dataSourceService:     dataSourceService,
		pluginSettingsService: pluginSettingsService,
		logger:                log.New("plugin.context"),
	}
}

type Provider struct {
	*BaseProvider
	cacheService          *localcache.CacheService
	pluginStore           pluginstore.Store
	dataSourceCache       datasources.CacheService
	dataSourceService     datasources.DataSourceService
	pluginSettingsService pluginsettings.Service
	logger                log.Logger

  "text": "The Grafana datasource plugin query path in grafana/grafana involves three major layers:\n\n## 1. Query Request Handler (HTTP Layer)\n\n**`pkg/api/ds_query.go`** is the entry point. The route `POST /ds/query` is registered in `pkg/api/api.go` (line 506) and mapped to `getDSQueryEndpoint()`. This function checks the `FlagQueryServiceRewrite` feature flag:\n- If enabled, it rewrites requests to `/apis/query.grafana.app/v0alpha1/namespaces/{namespace}/query` and serves them via the Kubernetes API machinery (`clientConfigProvider.DirectlyServeHTTP`).\n- Otherwise, it calls `QueryMetricsV2`, which binds the incoming HTTP body as a `dtos.MetricRequest` (defined in `pkg/api/dtos/models.go`), delegates to `queryDataService.QueryData` or `QueryDataNew`, and returns the response via `toJsonStreamingResponse` which streams JSON back to the client.\n\n## 2. Query Service Core Logic\n\n**`pkg/services/query/query.go`** contains the `ServiceImpl` which implements the `Service` interface. It:\n- `parseMetricRequest`: parses queries grouped by datasource UID, resolves datasource info (from cache or service), and builds `backend.DataQuery` objects.\n- `handleQuerySingleDatasource`: for single-datasource queries, builds a `backend.QueryDataRequest`, retrieves the plugin context via `plugincontext.Provider.GetWithDataSource`, and dispatches to the `plugins.Client`.\n- `executeConcurrentQueries`: for multi-datasource requests, fans out queries concurrently using `errgroup`.\n- `handleExpressions`: routes expression queries through the expression service (`expr.Service.TransformData`).\n- It supports two routing modes: single-tenant (direct plugin call) and query-service/multi-tenant (via `QSDatasourceClientBuilder` which calls the k8s query service API).\n\n**`pkg/services/query/models.go`** defines `parsedRequest` and `parsedQuery` (internal query parsing types).\n**`pkg/services/query/errors.go`** defines typed errors for the query path.\n\n## 3. Kubernetes Query API (Alternative/New Path)\n\nWhen `FlagQueryService` or `FlagQueryServiceRewrite` is enabled, the query is handled by:\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/register.go`**: `QueryAPIBuilder` + `RegisterAPIService` register the query API group with the Kubernetes API server.\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/query.go`**: `queryREST.Connect` is the HTTP handler called by the apiserver. It binds the request as a `query.QueryDataRequest`, calls `handleQuery`, which rebuilds a `dtos.MetricRequest` and calls `service.QueryData` (the same core query service).\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/client/plugin.go`**: `pluginClient.QueryData` performs access-control checks and dispatches to the internal `plugins.Client` with the datasource's plugin context.\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/client/instance_provider.go`**: `singleTenantInstanceProvider` wires the single-tenant plugin client.\n- **`pkg/registry/apis/query/clientapi/clientapi.go`**: Defines the `QueryDataClient`, `Instance`, and `InstanceProvider` interfaces.\n- **`pkg/services/dsquerierclient/qs_datasource_client_builder.go`**: `QSDatasourceClientBuilder` interface and implementations that bridge between the query service and the datasource client.\n\n## 4. Plugin Backend Query Dispatch\n\n**`pkg/plugins/manager/client/client.go`**: The `Service.QueryData` method looks up the plugin by ID from the plugin registry, validates it is not decommissioned, and calls `p.QueryData`.\n\n**Plugin implementations:**\n- **`pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/grpc_plugin.go`**: `grpcPlugin.QueryData` — dispatches to the gRPC client for external plugins.\n- **`pkg/plugins/backendplugin/grpcplugin/client_v2.go`**: `ClientV2.QueryData` — converts the request to protobuf via `backend.ToProto().QueryDataRequest()`, sends it over gRPC, and converts the response back via `backend.FromProto().QueryDataResponse()`.\n- **`pkg/plugins/backendplugin/coreplugin/core_plugin.go`**: `corePlugin.QueryData` — calls `QueryDataHandler.QueryData` directly for built-in plugins.\n- **`pkg/plugins/ifaces.go`**: The `Client` interface (alias for `backend.Handler`) that all plugin client implementations must satisfy.\n\n## 5. Plugin Middleware Chain\n\n**`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/pluginsintegration.go`**: `CreateMiddlewares` + `NewMiddlewareHandler` assemble the middleware stack that wraps every plugin call:\n1. `TracingMiddleware` (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/tracing_middleware.go`) — creates OpenTelemetry spans.\n2. `MetricsMiddleware` (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/metrics_middleware.go`) — records Prometheus metrics (request count, duration, size).\n3. `ContextualLoggerMiddleware` — contextual logging.\n4. `TracingHeaderMiddleware` — propagates tracing headers.\n5. `ClearAuthHeadersMiddleware` — clears sensitive headers.\n6. `OAuthTokenMiddleware` — injects OAuth tokens.\n7. `CookiesMiddleware` — forwards cookies.\n8. `CachingMiddleware` (`pkg/services/pluginsintegration/clientmiddleware/caching_middleware.go`) — cache hit/miss logic for query responses.\n9. `ForwardIDMiddleware`, `UseAlertHeadersMiddleware`, `UserHeaderMiddleware`, `HTTPClientMiddleware`.\n10. `ErrorSourceMiddleware` — classifies errors as plugin-source vs downstream.\n\n## 6.
